title: Declare and initialise arrays
successMessage: scores.length is 5 but the last index is 4 — arrays are 0-indexed. Off-by-one errors (using .length instead of .length-1) cause ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException — a very common Java bug.

title: Loop over arrays
instruction:
Two ways to iterate:

1. Classic `for` with index — when you need the position
2. For-each — when you just need each value

Print each score with its index.
hint: for (int i = 0; i < scores.length; i++) { System.out.println(i + ": " + scores[i]); }

codeChecks.message: Use a classic for loop with index i.

successMessage: Classic for with index — the most common array traversal pattern in Java. You'll write this hundreds of times.

title: Find min, max, sum
instruction:
Calculate statistics by looping through the array manually.

Write code to find the sum, min, and max of the scores array.
hint: Start sum at 0, min at scores[0], max at scores[0]. Loop through and update.

codeChecks.message: Track minimum: if (s < min) min = s;
Track maximum: if (s > max) max = s;

successMessage: Sum, min, max, average — the four statistics every data processing program needs. Notice (double) sum / scores.length — the cast prevents integer division.

title: Arrays utility class
instruction:
`java.util.Arrays` provides ready-made operations:

- `Arrays.sort(arr)` — sort in place
- `Arrays.toString(arr)` — print as string
- `Arrays.copyOf(arr, newLength)` — copy with different size
- `Arrays.fill(arr, value)` — fill with a value
- `Arrays.binarySearch(arr, value)` — fast search (requires sorted array)

Run this.
successMessage: Arrays.toString() for printing, Arrays.sort() for sorting, binarySearch() for fast lookup. These are the workhorses of array operations in Java.

title: ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException
instruction:
Access an index that doesn't exist and Java throws `ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException`. It's one of the most common Java runtime errors.

Fix the three out-of-bounds accesses.
hint: Array has indices 0, 1, 2, 3, 4 for length 5. scores.length-1 is the last valid index.
successMessage: scores.length - 1 is always the safe last index. Forgetting this -1 causes AIOOBE — one of Java's most common bugs. Always double-check loop conditions and index access.

title: 2D arrays — matrices
instruction:
2D arrays are arrays of arrays — perfect for grids, tables, and matrices:

```java
int[][] grid = new int[3][3];
int[][] matrix = {{1,2,3}, {4,5,6}, {7,8,9}};
```

Access with `matrix[row][col]`. Iterate with nested loops.

Build and print a 3×3 identity matrix.
hint: int[][] id = new int[3][3]; for (int i = 0; i < 3; i++) id[i][i] = 1;
successMessage: A 3×3 identity matrix. int[][] is an array of int arrays. The nested for-each loops over rows, then columns. 2D arrays power image processing, game boards, and mathematical computations.

title: Build a grade analyser
instruction:
Final challenge — no starter code.

Given `int[] grades = {72, 88, 95, 64, 81, 77, 90, 55, 83, 69}`:

1. Print the sorted grades
2. Print min and max
3. Print the average to 1 decimal place
4. Count how many are passing (>= 60)
5. Print `"PASS: X/10 students"`
hint: Use Arrays.sort(), a loop for stats, count++ for passing grades.

codeChecks.message: Use Arrays.sort() to sort the grades.

successMessage: A complete grade analyser — sort, stats, count. This is the foundation of every reporting system. Chapter 7 done!
